title: Schnorr signature domain separation vulnerability
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/link/schnorr48.c
Lines: 291-306, 393-400

The Schnorr-48 frame signature lacks domain separation between address and payload fields. The signed message is constructed as:

```c
header[header_len++] = epoch;
header[header_len++] = (uint8_t)(seqnum >> 8);
header[header_len++] = (uint8_t)(seqnum & 0xFF);
if (dst_addr_len > 0) {
    memcpy(&header[header_len], dst_addr, dst_addr_len);
    header_len += dst_addr_len;
}
// ... hash(R || pubkey || header || payload)
```

Without length prefixes, the same hash is produced for different (dst_addr, payload) combinations:

- Frame A: addr_mode=EUI64, dst_addr=0x0102030405060708, payload=0x0A0B
- Frame B: addr_mode=BROADCAST, payload=0x01020304050607080A0B

Both produce SHA-512(R || pubkey || epoch || seqnum || 0x01...080A0B).

Attack scenario for CRC32-MIC mode:
1. Capture signed unicast frame
2. Reparse as broadcast with extended payload
3. Recompute CRC32 (no key needed)
4. Signature still verifies

Mitigation: AES-CCM-64 MIC prevents this attack since the attacker cannot recompute the keyed MAC. For CRC32-MIC mode (already documented as insecure), this is exploitable.

Fix: Add dst_addr_len as a prefix in the signed message:
```c
header[header_len++] = (uint8_t)dst_addr_len;  // Domain separation
```
notes:
Commit: 7adbe0cf0
Added dst_addr_len prefix byte in header construction for both schnorr48_sign_frame() and schnorr48_verify_frame() (lines ~299, ~405). Updated header[13]->[14] and comments. This provides domain separation preventing addr/payload ambiguity. (Note: this changes all signatures; test vectors and Python reference will need update in follow-up bead.)
